vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
generated_parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

verify.rule: All required evidence_features are present in part_tree/geometry_ops.
The estimated mass matches the provided CAD brief.
Each physical feature in the geometry_ops can be traced back to its corresponding ID in the part_tree.
Adjacent interfaces are reciprocal with matching connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to insufficient cooling ducts.
risk.mit: Integrate additional cooling ducts to ensure proper airflow.

risk.desc: Risk of landing gear failure due to suboptimal mount point design.
risk.mit: Optimize landing gear mount points for enhanced structural integrity.

risk.desc: GPS boom mounting may require additional support for stability.
risk.mit: Streamline GPS boom mounting to reduce assembly time and improve stability.

risk.desc: Camera window may obstruct critical view angles if not properly positioned.
risk.mit: Ensure camera window placement allows for unobstructed views.

risk.desc: Service access panel design may limit maintenance accessibility.
risk.mit: Review and optimize service access panel design for better maintenance access.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads routed through wing spar socket into fuselage spine
Avionics cooling airflow through ducts to external vents
Heat transfer from avionics components to heat sink fins
Tail control surface actuation forces transmitted through structure
Landing gear compression forces transmitted to leg mounts

manufacturing_strategy.datum: primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.support: horizontal orientation during FDM, vertical for CNC
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and CMM for dimensional accuracy

generated_parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

requirements: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Structural integrity for nacelle loads into fuselage spine
V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

verify.rule: mass budget

verify.check: All load paths from nacelle to fuselage spine are intact and supported
verify.rule: load path integrity

verify.check: All necessary service access points are clear and accessible
verify.rule: service access

verify.check: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
verify.rule: interface_count

verify.check: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
verify.rule: clearance_named

verify.check: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references
verify.rule: datum_visibility

verify.check: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
verify.rule: standard_hardware_dimensioning

verify.check: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible
verify.rule: tilt_axis_readability

verify.check: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable
verify.rule: wing_fuselage_interface

risk.desc: Insufficient visibility of primary locating faces and interfaces may lead to misalignment during assembly.
risk.mit: Ensure all primary locating faces and interfaces are clearly visible and labeled in the design.

risk.desc: Potential misinterpretation of tilt axis and bearing seat locations could affect nacelle installation.
risk.mit: Add detailed annotations and visual aids to clarify the location of the tilt axis and bearing seats.

risk.desc: Improper service access might hinder maintenance and repair operations.
risk.mit: Review and ensure all service access points are clear and easily accessible.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.path: Nacelle loads -> Wing spar -> Fuselage spine
Heat dissipation from electronics to wing skin
Pitch and roll movement around fuselage spine
Power and data lines from fuselage to wing

opportunities: Carry loads from nacelle into fuselage spine
Allow wing spar to connect to fuselage spine

manufacturing_strategy.process: CNC milling for spars and sockets, FDM for TPU parts
manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.1mm for structural elements, ±0.5mm for service access
manufacturing_strategy.support: Orient parts for optimal tool access, ensure support structures for FDM
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment, CMM for dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.check: All load paths from nacelle to fuselage spine are intact and supported
All necessary service access points are clear and accessible

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.

verify.rule: Ensure all required fields are present and correctly formatted.
Ensure the mass estimate does not exceed the allocated budget.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to press-fit tolerance.
risk.mit: Use precision manufacturing techniques and ensure tight quality control.

risk.desc: Risk of pushrod damage during maintenance if service access is inadequate.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service access cut to allow easy pushrod replacement.

risk.desc: Crash tolerance may be compromised if ball joints are not properly reinforced.
risk.mit: Add additional reinforcement around ball joints to improve structural integrity.

risk.desc: Mass estimation may be inaccurate if support structures are not accounted for.
risk.mit: Conduct a detailed analysis of support structures and their impact on final part mass.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance between components may cause mechanical interference.
risk.mit: Review and adjust component clearances to ensure proper fit and function.

risk.mit: Increase clearance to 1mm for bolted connections.

risk.mit: Use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.
